Refactor Bash credential-file checks (lines 161-198) from individual inline regex+if blocks into a BLOCKED_BASH_PATHS array with a loop, matching the pattern already used for Read tool BLOCKED_PATHS (D4)
Anchor env/printenv checks to handle mid-command positions (e.g., cd /tmp && env) by scanning for word boundaries rather than start-of-string (D1)
Add stderr logging (>&2 echo) when blocking actions to enable auditing and debugging in production (D3)
Anchor the `uv run (ruff|pre-commit)` pattern with ^ to prevent false positives on commands that contain the string mid-command (e.g., echo statements)
Add documented test cases in the header for linting, docker, and uv sync patterns -- currently only pytest cases are documented
Add stderr logging on blocks (e.g., >&2 echo) to aid debugging in production
Add pipe `|` as a separator in PYTHON_RE alongside `&&`, `;`, `||` -- commands like `echo test | python script.py` bypass the hook because single pipe is not matched (D1)
Adopt the PATTERNS array approach (like enforce-make-commands.sh) for extensibility -- currently a single pattern; adding pip/pipx blocking would require duplicating logic (D4)
Add stderr logging on blocks (>&2 echo) for auditing and debugging in production (D3)
Fix GNU sed portability: replace s/^-\|-$//g alternation with two separate sed expressions (s/^-//; s/-$//) for macOS compatibility -- D3/D4
Enrich HOOKS.md entry to document slug generation algorithm, CLAUDE_PLANS_DIR env var, and timestamp-based naming convention -- D6
Add test cases for edge cases in header comments: empty title (fallback slug), special characters in title, and cp failure scenarios -- D2
[low] Extract LEARNED_FILE path into a configurable env var (e.g. CLAUDE_LEARNED_FILE) for consistency with CLAUDE_MEMORIES_DIR -- D4
[low] Replace ls/xargs/sed/grep pipeline (line 53) with a cleaner glob-based for-loop approach for listing non-essential memories -- D4
[low] Add stderr logging (e.g. >&2 echo to /tmp/session-start.log) when errors are suppressed, to aid debugging -- D3

[med] Extract dangerous patterns into arrays/variables for extensibility -- inline patterns in if-blocks make adding new rules harder than necessary; compare to block-config-edits.sh BLOCKED_CONFIGS array (D4)
[med] Add stderr logging for blocked commands to enable auditing -- currently no way to track what was blocked and when (D3)
[low] Consolidate -rf/-fr flag ordering duplication into a single regex or helper function -- the dual regex is repeated 3 times across root/home/cwd checks (D4)
Handle non-existent files more gracefully -- allow Read to fail naturally instead of blocking with a /read-json suggestion for files that don't exist
Expand the allowlist to include more common small JSON config files (renovate.json, biome.json, .babelrc.json, deno.json) or consider an allowlist pattern for dotfile JSON (.*rc.json)
Add failure logging (stderr) when jq parsing fails, so issues are diagnosable without silent pass-through

[med] Fix Bash regex false positives and missing single-redirect -- APPEND_RE's greedy `>>.*HOME_CONFIG` matches jq/heredoc commands containing config paths in string literals (confirmed live: hook blocked a jq command writing evaluations.json because the JSON payload contained .bashrc), and single `>` redirect is not caught at all (D1/D3)
[low] Unify BLOCKED_CONFIGS array and Bash CONFIGS regex into a single source of truth -- currently two independent lists that can drift when adding new config files (D4)
[low] Add documented test cases in header comments (block and allow examples) matching the pattern in block-dangerous-commands.sh -- currently no inline test commands (D2/D5)

Update HOOKS.md index to replace stale enforce-feature-branch.sh entry with git-safety.sh, and differentiate block_severe/block_soft implementations so the two-tier severity model is reflected in actual output (D6, D4)
Add stderr logging (>&2 echo) when blocks fire to enable audit trails and production debugging (D3)
Unify push-target extraction into a single parse pass instead of re-grepping COMMAND multiple times for --delete, :branch, and cross-branch patterns (D4)
